title: microgramma lycopodioides f. subdimorpha
intro: Microgramma lycopodioides f. subdimorpha, commonly known as 'Rabbit's Foot Fern', is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Microgramma lycopodioides f. subdimorpha include its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents present in this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.

side_effects: Microgramma lycopodioides f. subdimorpha can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps, due to its toxic compounds.
